oil_price_shock.name: Oil Price Shock
oil_price_shock.detail: Oil prices surged 43% since late February as Iran closed Strait of Hormuz. Brent above $104, WTI near $99.

hormuz_flow.name: Strait of Hormuz Flow
hormuz_flow.detail: Strait completely closed for 15+ days. Iran enforcing total blockade. 20% of global oil transit eliminated. US Navy in active combat operations.

vix_fear.name: VIX Fear Gauge
vix_fear.detail: VIX at 24, up from 19.9 in late February. Futures pricing in continued volatility above 28.

defense_momentum.name: Defense Sector Momentum
defense_momentum.detail: Defense stocks hitting 52-week highs. LMT +37% in 3 months, RTX +62% over year. 'Operation Epic Fury' driving massive weapons demand.

gold_safe_haven.name: Gold Safe Haven Flow
gold_safe_haven.detail: Gold at unprecedented ~$5,020/oz reflecting extreme monetary debasement fears and inflation from energy crisis.

diplomatic_signals.name: Diplomatic Activity
diplomatic_signals.detail: Diplomatic channels collapsed. Iran's Supreme Leader killed. Trump demanding regime change. China's mediation efforts failed.

military_posture.name: Military Posture
military_posture.detail: Day 15 of 'Operation Epic Fury'. US-Israel conducting daily airstrikes on Iranian oil infrastructure, nuclear sites, military bases. Iran retaliating.

OVERWEIGHT.rationale: Historic supply shock from Hormuz closure driving oil to $100+. Domestic producers major beneficiaries.
Active war driving unprecedented weapons consumption. Defense stocks hitting all-time highs.
Ultimate safe haven during wartime. Gold at historic highs on currency debasement fears.
Zero Middle East exposure, maximum benefit from $100+ oil prices

UNDERWEIGHT.rationale: Jet fuel costs surged 40%+. Middle East routes completely shut. Major airline bankruptcies possible.
Massive risk-off rotation to commodities/defense. Tech multiples collapsing in inflationary environment.
Energy cost surge destroying consumer discretionary spending power
Global shipping disrupted. Alternative routes 40%+ longer. Insurance costs prohibitive.

MONITOR.rationale: China economy devastated by oil import disruption but some benefit from Iran trade
Mixed exposure - Gulf assets at risk but benefiting from higher prices

scenarios.name: Regime Change Success
scenarios.description: Iranian government collapses, new regime reopens Hormuz within 2-4 weeks
scenarios.market_impact: Sharp risk-on rotation back to tech/growth. Defense stocks give back gains.
scenarios.positioning: Lock in energy/defense profits, prepare to rotate back to growth

scenarios.name: Prolonged Conflict
scenarios.description: War continues 2-6 months, Hormuz partially reopens with heavy military escort
scenarios.market_impact: Sustained inflation, Fed forced to hike rates. Recession risk high.
scenarios.positioning: Maximum overweight energy/defense/gold. Underweight everything else.

scenarios.name: Regional War Expansion
scenarios.description: China/Russia military intervention, global conflict, complete energy system breakdown
scenarios.positioning: Physical assets only: gold, oil, defense. Exit financial assets entirely.

companies.name: ExxonMobil
companies.exposure: Qatar North Field LNG operations suspended

companies.name: Lockheed Martin
companies.exposure: Massive weapons orders from Operation Epic Fury

companies.name: RTX/Raytheon
companies.exposure: Patriot missile systems in heavy demand

companies.name: ZIM Shipping
companies.exposure: Israeli flag carrier, routes completely disrupted

companies.name: PetroChina/CNPC
companies.exposure: Buying Iranian oil at massive discounts

companies.name: Sinopec
companies.exposure: Iran oil imports via overland routes

china_russia_iran.dependency: China economy severely impacted but some energy companies benefit from discounted Iran oil
